name: Fruit Hints
recipeInstructions:
I'm not at all good with desserts. My favorite thing after a big meal is fruit. I know, you're going to say "how boring!" Just give me a chance. I have some tricks in my apron pocket! Here are a few ideas:
GRAPES: Why not run them under cold water, dust with regular sugar, and put them in the freezer for an hour? They won't last long enough on your table to defrost. Try mixing a bowl of different colored grapes.
PEACHES: Take about 1 peach per person, especially in the winter. Slice them in wedges, sprinkle with cinnamon, add a tablespoon of some liqueur (any flavor), and cover. Cook in the microwave for 5-7 minutes. Serve as you would applesauce with pork roast; it's great with any roast. You can also make a mixture of fruits for this, including pear, nectarine, plums...use your imagination.
STRAWBERRIES: The season is coming soon and I want you all prepared for this one. It's the best. Wash strawberries well, then take off the top or any bruises (you don't want to wash away any flavor). Cut each one in half or thirds if needed. Place in a large bowl with a secure cover or a large jar. Add a few tablespoons of sugar and the juice of a fresh lemon or two. Fill your container nearly covering the berries, place the cover on, and shake gently. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours, turning and shaking every hour or so. No need to serve anything else; just provide juice glasses and a ladle when the berries are done. Then sit back and relax.
